task: Write a Python function balanced(s) returning True when the parentheses in the string are balanced, considering only the characters '(' and ')' and ignoring everything else. A closing parenthesis must never appear before its opening partner: ')(' is not balanced. An empty string is balanced. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles an empty string as balanced.
trace: The function `balanced(s)` uses a stack data structure to keep track of the opening parentheses. For every opening parenthesis encountered, it gets pushed onto the stack. When a closing parenthesis is found, if the stack is empty or the top element of the stack is not an opening parenthesis, the function returns `False`. If no such case occurs and the stack is empty at the end, the function returns `True`, indicating that the parentheses are balanced. An edge case handled by this solution is when there are nested parentheses.
